summary: Exceptional test suite. Coverage is comprehensive: every public function (load_secrets, calculate_secret_status, analyze_secrets, format_markdown, format_json) is tested, and all task requirements (expiry detection, warning window, urgency grouping, both output formats, error handling, file I/O) are exercised. Rigor is outstanding — boundary conditions are explicitly tested (secret expiring exactly on the warning boundary, negative days_until_expiry, empty input, all-ok scenario), error paths are covered with specific exception type and message assertions, and a smoke test validates behavior when reference_date is None. Design is excellent: tests are organized into clearly labeled TDD-cycle classes with a shared REFERENCE_DATE constant ensuring determinism, pytest fixtures avoid repetition in format tests, test names are descriptive and intention-revealing, and test independence is maintained throughout. The only minor gap is the absence of integration/CLI-level tests for the main() entry point (e.g., argument parsing, exit codes), but the unit coverage is thorough enough that the overall suite would reliably catch regressions across all core logic paths.
